Title: Post-Cyclization Skeletal Rearrangements in Plant Triterpenoid Biosynthesis by a Pair of Branchpoint Isomerases
Journal: Journal of the American Chemical Society

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: isomeliandiol; protoglabretal; apoprotolimonoid/limonoid/quassinoid and glabretane triterpenoid precursors
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: specialized triterpenoid scaffold diversification; industrially relevant limonoid/quassinoid/glabretane biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highly and predominantly expressed in roots
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: epoxidizes the C-7,8 double bond of melianol to form 7,8-epoxymelianol
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: midstream branch-generating step after initial cyclization and early oxidations
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor for rearranged triterpenoid branches
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point; central step enabling pathway bifurcation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes formation of 7,8-epoxymelianol and thereby enables downstream production of isomeliandiol and protoglabretal class triterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AaCYP88A154 was the only oxidoreductase candidate that consistently consumed melianol in the heterologous screen. 2. It generated 7,8-epoxymelianol, a previously unknown unstable biosynthetic intermediate. 3. This epoxidation is a central step underlying apoprotolimonoid, limonoid, quassinoid, and glabretane biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC-MS metabolite profiling; compound purification and NMR; chemical mimicry by m-CPBA epoxidation; in vitro microsome assay in Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq expression/co-expression analysis from de novo transcriptome sequencing of Ailanthus altissima; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: heterologous expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; chemical epoxidation with m-CPBA for comparison
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful for metabolic engineering of limonoid, quassinoid, apoprotolimonoid, and glabretane triterpenoids; essential enzyme to install the rearrangement-triggering epoxide before branch-selective isomerases are introduced
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Products and intermediate are acid-labile; close homologues occur in Citrus sinensis and Citrus grandis, supporting broader Sapindales relevance.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: post-cyclization skeletal rearrangement toward apoprotolimonoid branch
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: sterol isomerase-like protein related to C-8,7 sterol isomerases
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expression profile highly similar to triterpenoid pathway genes; root-associated
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: channels rearrangement of 7,8-epoxymelianol toward isomeliandiol through nonoxidative skeletal isomerization
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms branch-defining rearranged scaffold precursor
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: shifts product profile strongly toward isomeliandiol formation from the shared epoxide intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Co-expression of AaISM1 with upstream genes caused a strong shift toward isomeliandiol as the major product. 2. AaISM1 uses the shared epoxide intermediate generated by AaCYP88A154. 3. The enzyme acts as a central gatekeeper for the apoprotolimonoid/limonoid/quassinoid branch.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC-MS product redistribution analysis; in vitro microsome assay using heterologous Saccharomyces cerevisiae expression
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq expression/co-expression analysis from de novo transcriptome sequencing of Ailanthus altissima; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: valuable branch-selective enzyme for engineering limonoid/quassinoid-related scaffolds after cyclization; can be combined with upstream epoxidase for controlled scaffold editing
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although grouped here as a pathway gene due to direct catalytic rearrangement, it is an isomerase rather than a classic oxidoreductase; evolved from primary-metabolism sterol isomerases.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: post-cyclization skeletal rearrangement toward glabretane branch
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: sterol isomerase-like protein related to C-8,7 sterol isomerases
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expression profile highly similar to triterpenoid pathway genes; root-associated
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes nonoxidative skeletal rearrangement of 7,8-epoxymelianol to protoglabretal, installing a cyclopropane-containing glabretane scaffold
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms branch-defining rearranged scaffold precursor
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: causes disappearance of the epoxide intermediate and formation of protoglabretal, defining the glabretane branch
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Co-expression of AaISM2 with upstream genes almost completely consumed 7,8-epoxymelianol and produced a new product, protoglabretal. 2. In vitro assays showed AaISM2 accepts 7,8-epoxymelianol but not isomeliandiol, supporting direct branch formation from the shared epoxide. 3. AaISM2 is a central gatekeeper for glabretane scaffold biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC-MS metabolite profiling; compound purification and NMR; in vitro microsome assay using heterologous Saccharomyces cerevisiae expression; substrate specificity test
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq expression/co-expression analysis from de novo transcriptome sequencing of Ailanthus altissima; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful for engineering cyclopropane-containing glabretane scaffolds and expanding triterpenoid structural diversity in biotechnology
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents a novel route for enzymatic cyclopropane installation onto an existing triterpenoid skeleton; evolved from sterol isomerase ancestry.
